{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether antibiotics decrease post-tonsillectomy morbidity. Reviewing the documents, particularly Document [4] from the MEDCORP source, states explicitly: 'Clinicians should not routinely administer or prescribe perioperative antibiotics to children undergoing tonsillectomy. [Level I]' This indicates that routine use of antibiotics before or after tonsillectomy is not recommended. Furthermore, the documents emphasize that antibiotics are only indicated for confirmed bacterial tonsillitis (e.g., GAS) and are not routinely used to reduce postoperative complications. There is no evidence in the provided documents that antibiotics reduce post-tonsillectomy morbidity such as hemorrhage, infection, or pain. In fact, the guidelines caution against routine antibiotic use to prevent antimicrobial resistance and unnecessary side effects. Therefore, based on the evidence, antibiotics do not decrease post-tonsillectomy morbidity.", "answer_choice": "B"}
